Just now

Stop bottlenecking your AI workloads. Learn how to configure a bare-metal software stack for maximum compute efficiency.
Press enter or click to view image in full size

If you are diving into deep learning or training Large Language Models (LLMs), a GPU dedicated server provides the bare-metal performance required to maximize compute efficiency. But hardware is only half the battle. To harness the full power of an RTX 3090, 4090, or enterprise GPU, you need a flawless software stack.
In our latest technical breakdown at Fit Servers, we walk you through the exact pipeline for setting up an Ubuntu 22.04 server for heavy AI workloads.
Here is a sneak peek at the workflow:

• The Clean Slate: Before installing anything, we show you how to purge pre-installed, conflicting packages.

• The Foundation: Installing the NVIDIA Driver (v560) via the official PPA.

• The Tooling: Installing CUDA 12.4 and cuDNN 9 — critical for compiling custom CUDA kernels and accelerating deep learning primitives.

• The Environment: Using Miniconda to prevent dependency hell.

• The Execution: Installing PyTorch with GPU support and running a custom matrix multiplication benchmark to prove your TFLOPS are where they should be.
Ready to scale your AI projects and eliminate virtualization overhead?
👉 Read the complete guide and grab the setup commands on the Fit Servers blog!
